LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GENE TYGIELSKI, SAMIR KHOURY'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS GENE TYGIELSKI, SAMIR KHOURY'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE MOTION OF TYGIELSKI IS DENIED. HE HAD NOTICE OF THE SUIT AND THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT ESTABLISH EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE MOTION OF KHOURY IS GRANTED. HIS ILLNESS PREVENTED HIM FROM RESPONDING TO THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT TO FILE HIS ANSWER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PURSUANT TO REQUEST BY MOVING PARTY, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT GENE TYGIELSKI'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS ORDERED CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 13, 2008. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)

Off Calendar Sep-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard E. Paris, Esq. (415-456-0678), appearing for defendants / moving parties Gene L. Tygielski and Samir Khoury via courtcall
Lina M. Michael, Esq. of BERKE & ASSOCIATES 805-379-8508, appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Leaf Funding, Inc. via courtcall

Ruling:
DEFENDANTS GENE TYGIELSKI, SAMIR KHOURY'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS GENE TYGIELSKI, SAMIR KHOURY'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment. The motion of Tygielski is denied. He had notice of the suit and the facts alleged do not establish excusable neglect. The motion of Khoury is granted. His illness prevented him from responding to the complaint. Defendant to file his answer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-04-2008 Continued To Nov-13-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Ruling:
Pursuant to request by moving party, Counsel for DEFENDANT GENE TYGIELSKI'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is ordered continued to November 13, 2008. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)
